title: phaseolus vulgaris var. lardinus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. lardinus, also known as Larden's garden bean or Larden's bean, is a variety of the common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) and is considered a type of legume. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Phaseolus vulgaris var. lardinus include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of certain diseases, such as heart disease and diabetes. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, capsules, or infusions, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
